meta_description: Learn the differences between polyacetal (POM) and Delrin and when to use each plastic in CNC machining or injection molding.

sections.heading: Manufacturing with Polyacetal vs. Delrin
sections.text: Manufacturing with Polyacetal vs. Delrin Polyacetal vs. Delrin: How to choose the right POM plastic for manufacturing

sections.heading: Properties of polyacetal√Ç
sections.text: Great parts start with the right material and when it comes to precision plastics, polyacetal (POM) and Delrin are at the top of the game. Both are types of polyoxymethylene, an engineering-grade thermoplastic known for its strength, stiffness, and low friction. They√¢¬Ä¬ôre commonly used in CNC machining and injection molding because they hold tight tolerances and produce smooth surface finishes. They√¢¬Ä¬ôre rarely used in 3D printing due to high shrinkage and warping, which make them difficult to print reliably.√Ç The base polymer, POM, was studied as early as the 1920s, but it wasn√¢¬Ä¬ôt until the 1950s that DuPont developed a new version using end-capping technology. By chemically sealing off t...
